claim: Writing agent skills that actually improve performance requires systematic testing with evaluations—most intuitively-written skills don't measurably help or actively hurt agent behavior
actionable: Test every skill with a before/after eval harness; measure agent performance with and without the skill rather than assuming good descriptions will improve behavior
source_title: Skill Issue: How We Used AI to Make Agents Actually Good at Supabase — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase

claim: Skills vs MCP are complementary, not competing: use MCP for integrations and tools, use skills for progressive disclosure of context and instructions
actionable: Implement integrations as MCP servers; wrap them in skills that provide usage instructions, domain knowledge, and progressive disclosure of what the MCP tools do
source_title: Skill Issue: How We Used AI to Make Agents Actually Good at Supabase — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase

claim: Using the verb 'use' at the start of a skill description increases agent loading likelihood by ~2x (at least on Claude models)
actionable: Begin skill descriptions with 'Use <skill-name> to...' rather than passive descriptions; test loading rates with and without this pattern
source_title: Skill Issue: How We Used AI to Make Agents Actually Good at Supabase — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase

claim: Progressive disclosure in skills means the front matter (name, description) is loaded always, but full content is only loaded when the agent chooses to use the skill
actionable: Structure skills with a concise front matter description (~1-2 sentences) and detailed implementation in the body; rely on agent discretion to load full content
source_title: Skill Issue: How We Used AI to Make Agents Actually Good at Supabase — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase

claim: Agent skills should be treated as production documentation, not one-off fixes—keep them updated when product features change, or deprecate them
actionable: Include skill maintenance in your product documentation workflow; create CI checks to detect stale skills (not loaded by agents for N days)
source_title: Skill Issue: How We Used AI to Make Agents Actually Good at Supabase — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase

claim: When evaluating skills, use deterministic assertions where possible (e.g., 'flag X is present in the SQL') rather than LLM judges, to avoid false negatives from hallucination
actionable: Build eval assertions around verifiable facts (SQL flags, function calls, schema changes) and reserve LLM judging for soft goals like clarity or correctness
source_title: Skill Issue: How We Used AI to Make Agents Actually Good at Supabase — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase

claim: Skills with checklist-like instructions (do A, then do B) can mislead agents into busywork; focus on high-level goals and let agents decide implementation
actionable: Write skill instructions as goals, not step-by-step procedures; avoid prescriptive lists that force agents to follow unnecessary steps
source_title: Skill Issue: How We Used AI to Make Agents Actually Good at Supabase — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase

claim: Teach agents security patterns (e.g., Postgres RLS, security-invoker flag) via skills, not LLM memorization—agents miss domain-specific gotchas without explicit instruction
actionable: Create domain-specific skills for security patterns, DB-specific flags, and edge cases in your product; include concrete examples and reference documentation
source_title: Skill Issue: How We Used AI to Make Agents Actually Good at Supabase — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase

claim: Skills can reference other skills and files to form a knowledge graph; use this to decompose complex domains into modular, reusable chunks
actionable: Structure skills hierarchically: have a top-level skill reference sub-skills for different subsystems or domains; include cross-references in markdown
source_title: Skill Issue: How We Used AI to Make Agents Actually Good at Supabase — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase

claim: Scripts in skills are environment-dependent (Linux vs macOS vs Windows); avoid them for cross-platform use or rely on platform detection
actionable: For cross-platform support, use MCP tools instead of shell scripts in skills, or explicitly document environment requirements
source_title: Skill Issue: How We Used AI to Make Agents Actually Good at Supabase — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase
